{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}40|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yoshiaki Miyake|year=1989|chapter=Kohasu {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=Kōchi Prefecture's Historical Sites|series={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}40|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yoshiaki Miyake|year=1989|chapter=Kohasu Kofun|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Archaeological Research Report Volume 3|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|isbn=|ref={{Harv|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006}}}} – Link via Nara National Research Institute for {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=Kōchi Prefecture's Historical Sites|series={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}40|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yoshiaki Miyake|year=1989|chapter=Kohasu Kofun|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Archaeological Research Report Volume 3|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|isbn=|ref={{Harv|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006}}}} – Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Funaiwa Kofun Cluster|ja|舟岩古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kohasu Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:History of {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=Kōchi Prefecture's Historical Sites|series={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}40|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yoshiaki Miyake|year=1989|chapter=Kohasu Kofun|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Archaeological Research Report Volume 3|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|isbn=|ref={{Harv|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006}}}} – Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Funaiwa Kofun Cluster|ja|舟岩古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kohasu Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | shape = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=Kōchi Prefecture's Historical Sites|series={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}40|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yoshiaki Miyake|year=1989|chapter=Kohasu Kofun|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Archaeological Research Report Volume 3|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|isbn=|ref={{Harv|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006}}}} – Link via Nara National Research Institute for Cultural Properties. == Related Topics == * {{ill|Funaiwa Kofun Cluster|ja|舟岩古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kohasu Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Site Explanation Board (Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=Kōchi Prefecture's Historical Sites|series={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of book|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Archaeological Research Report Volume 3|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|isbn=|ref={{Harv|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006}}}} – Link via Nara National Research Institute for The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|Excavated artifacts
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue ware

{{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | construction_period = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Tumulus in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original shape is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-copper beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-scale stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): - Length: 7.50 meters - Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) - Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): - Length: 3.25 meters - Width: 1.43 meters - Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones.
