Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == Burial Facility == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Cultural Properties == === Kōchi Prefectural Cultural Properties === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * other_designation Explanation Board Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == other_designation == === Kōchi Prefectural other_designation === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * other_designation Explanation Board {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == other_designation == === Kōchi Prefectural other_designation === * other_designation ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated artifacts from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * other_designation Explanation Board Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | artifacts = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designated as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. Artifacts excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations

Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designated a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | latitude = 33.5993 | longitude = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial_facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | label_position = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been designation1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The dimensions are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_other_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was other_designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery

This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_other_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was other_designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_other_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was other_designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == other_designation == === Kōchi Prefectural other_designation === * other_designation ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * other_designation Explanation Board But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_other_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was other_designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == other_designation == === Kōchi Prefectural other_designation === * other_designation ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * other_designation Explanation Board Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_other_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was other_designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == other_designation == === Kōchi Prefectural other_designation === * other_designation ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * other_designation Explanation Board The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was other_designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun Period Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_other_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was other_designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. {{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_other_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was other_designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery

Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_other_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was other_designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]][[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]][[Category:qq]] on (CatAlot)
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_other_designation = Kōchi Prefectural other_designation "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been other_designation1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was other_designation1 a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural other_designation. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == other_designation == === Kōchi Prefectural other_designation === * other_designation ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * other_designation Explanation Board (Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=Kōchi Prefecture's other_designations|series={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}40|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yoshiaki Miyake|year=1989|chapter=Kohasu Kofun|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations

on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of
