Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}}.}} == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=Kōchi Prefecture's Historic Sites|series={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系}}40|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yoshiaki Miyake|year=1989|chapter=Kohasu Kofun|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Archaeological Research Report Volume 3|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|isbn=|ref={{Harv|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006}}}} – Link via Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site.== See Also == * {{ill|Funaiwa Kofun Cluster|ja|舟岩古墳群}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kohasu Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:pp error]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Nankoku, Kōchi]] [[Category:Kōchi Prefectural Historic Sites]] [[Category:Kohasu Kofun]] [[Category:Koren Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:New2]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Koren Kofun]] [[ja:小蓮古墳]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Koren Kofun}}

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations

Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations

The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations

It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations

History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=Kōchi Prefecture's Historic Sites|series={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}}40|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yoshiaki Miyake|year=1989|chapter=Kohasu Kofun|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Archaeological Research Report Volume 3|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|isbn=|ref={{Harv|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006}}}} – Link via Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site. == See Also == * {{ill|Funaiwa Kofun Cluster|ja|舟岩古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kohasu Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Koren Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| {{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}}.}} == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|12=draft}} (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic Site Explanation Board (Nankoku City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=Kōchi Prefecture's Historic Sites|series={{ill|Historical and Geographical Encyclopedia of Japan|ja|日本歴史地名大系|12=draft}}40|publisher={{ill|Heibonsha|ja|平凡社|12=draft}}|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Heibonsha)|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=Yoshiaki Miyake|year=1989|chapter=Kohasu Kofun|title=Encyclopedia of Infobox ancient site|publisher={{ill|Tokyodo Publishing|ja|東京堂出版|12=draft}}|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harv|Kohasu Kofun (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Archaeological Research Report Volume 3|publisher=Kōchi University Archaeology Department|isbn=|ref={{Harv|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006}}}} – Link via Nara National Research Institute for Historic Site. == See Also == * {{ill|Funaiwa Kofun Cluster|ja|舟岩古墳群|12=draft}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Kohasu Kofun}} * The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. {{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

revision.comment: 24 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:小蓮古墳 up to rev 103486035
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 小蓮古墳 |画像 = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
石室開口部 |所在地 = [[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町小蓮（字光り岩）{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} |緯度度 = 33|緯度分 = 35|緯度秒 = 57.58 |経度度 =133|経度分 = 37|経度秒 = 8.93 |ISO = JP-39 |形状 = 不明 |規模 = |埋葬施設 = 両袖式[[横穴式石室]] |築造時期 = [[6世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |出土品 = 副葬品多数・[[須恵器]] |史跡 = 高知県指定史跡「小蓮古墳」 |特記事項 = 土佐三大古墳の1つ |地図 = Japan Kochi |アイコン = }} '''小蓮古墳'''（こはすこふん）は、[[高知県]][[南国市]]岡豊町小蓮にある[[古墳]]。高知県指定史跡に指定されている。 [[朝倉古墳]]（[[高知市]]朝倉）・[[明見彦山古墳群|明見彦山1号墳]]（南国市明見）と合わせて「土佐三大古墳」と総称される。 == 概要 ==
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;text-align:center;font-size:85%;" |+土佐の横穴式石室古墳の変遷[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/10493 『古墳時代終末期の大型横穴式石室にみる瀬戸内とその周辺の政治的関係』高知大学考古学調査研究報告第10冊] 高知大学人文学部考古学研究室、2012年、pp.7-20（リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」）。{{PDFlink|[https://kaken.nii.ac.jp/ja/file/KAKENHI-PROJECT-22520768/22520768seika.pdf 科学研究費助成事業（科学研究費補助金）研究成果報告書「横穴式石室導入に見る南四国と瀬戸内の古墳展開の研究」]}}清家章、2013年。 !rowspan=2|須恵器型式!!rowspan=2|特徴!!colspan=2|主な古墳 |- !盟主墳!!その他 |- |TK10新相
-TK43||明見3号型石室導入|| ||[[明見彦山古墳群|明見彦山3号墳]]
[[長畝古墳群|長畝4号墳]]
蒲原山東1・2号墳 |- |TK43||舟岩型石室導入||[[伏原大塚古墳]]|| |- |TK209||舟岩型石室普及
風水的選地導入||'''小蓮古墳'''||[[舟岩古墳群|舟岩1・3・8号墳]]
[[明見彦山古墳群|明見彦山1号墳]]
一宮大塚古墳
三ツ塚下古墳
[[新改古墳]] |- |TK217||角塚型石室導入||[[朝倉古墳]]|| |}
南国市西部、比江山断層線の南斜面ふもとで標高20メートルほどの地に築造された古墳である丸善出版 編『47都道府県ご当地文化大百科・高知県』丸善出版株式会社、16頁。{{Sfn|小蓮古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）に墳丘測量調査・石室実測調査・石室発掘調査が実施されたほか、近年では[[2004年]]度（[[平成]]16年度）に石室3次元デジタル計測が実施されている{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}。 現状の墳形は楕円形で（築造当初の墳形は不明{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}）、長径（南北）28メートル・短径（東西）22メートル・高さ7.13メートルを測り、後世に一部が削平されている。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口する（[[明治]]時代からすでに開口していたという）。[[玄室]]・[[羨道]]を含めた石室全長は10.8メートルを測り、高知県下では最大規模になる。発掘調査では、副葬品として金銅製中空玉・金環・鉄刀子・鉄鏃・鉇・馬貝（辻金具・轡など）・[[須恵器]]（坏・蓋・直口蓋・直口壺など）が検出されている。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。一帯では特大型の石室になることから、高知平野における盟主墳に位置づけられる古墳になる{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}。 古墳域は[[1953年]]（昭和28年）に高知県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）1月29日、高知県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1972年]]（昭和47年）、墳丘測量調査・石室実測調査・石室発掘調査{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}。 * [[2005年]]（[[平成]]17年）2月10-18日、石室3次元デジタル計測（高知大学人文学部考古学研究室、[[2006年]]に報告）{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}。 * 石室全長：10.75メートル * 玄室：長さ7.50メートル、幅1.95メートル（奥壁付近）・2.18メートル（玄門付近）、高さ2.85メートル（奥壁付近）・2.84メートル（玄室中央付近）・2.56メートル（玄門付近） * 羨道：長さ3.25メートル、幅1.43メートル、高さ1.85メートル 玄室の床面には割石を敷く。 Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|玄門（羨道方向） Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|[[高知県立歴史民俗資料館]]展示。}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|出土須恵器
{{small|高知県立歴史民俗資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 高知県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 小蓮古墳 - 1953年（昭和28年）1月29日指定[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm 小蓮古墳]（高知県ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * [[高知県立歴史民俗資料館]]（南国市岡豊町八幡） - 小蓮古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（南国市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=高知県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]40|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|小蓮古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=三宅良明|year=1989|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|小蓮古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|series=高知大学考古学調査研究報告第3冊|publisher=高知大学人文学部考古学研究室|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[舟岩古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kohasu Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm 小蓮古墳] - 高知県ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:こはすこふん}} [[Category:高知県の古墳]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:高知県指定史跡]]

南国市西部、比江山断層線の南斜面ふもとで標高20メートルほどの地に築造された古墳である丸善出版 編『47都道府県ご当地文化大百科・高知県』丸善出版株式会社、16頁。{{Sfn|小蓮古墳（平凡社）|1983}}。[[1972年]]（[[昭和]]47年）に墳丘測量調査・石室実測調査・石室発掘調査が実施されたほか、近年では[[2004年]]度（[[平成]]16年度）に石室3次元デジタル計測が実施されている{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}。 現状の墳形は楕円形で（築造当初の墳形は不明{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}）、長径（南北）28メートル・短径（東西）22メートル・高さ7.13メートルを測り、後世に一部が削平されている。埋葬施設は両袖式の[[横穴式石室]]で、南西方向に開口する（[[明治]]時代からすでに開口していたという）。[[玄室]]・[[羨道]]を含めた石室全長は10.8メートルを測り、高知県下では最大規模になる。発掘調査では、副葬品として金銅製中空玉・金環・鉄刀子・鉄鏃・鉇・馬貝（辻金具・轡など）・[[須恵器]]（坏・蓋・直口蓋・直口壺など）が検出されている。 築造時期は、[[古墳時代]]後期の[[6世紀]]後半頃と推定される。一帯では特大型の石室になることから、高知平野における盟主墳に位置づけられる古墳になる{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}。 古墳域は[[1953年]]（昭和28年）に高知県指定史跡に指定されている。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1953年]]（[[昭和]]28年）1月29日、高知県指定史跡に指定。 * [[1972年]]（昭和47年）、墳丘測量調査・石室実測調査・石室発掘調査{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}。 * [[2005年]]（[[平成]]17年）2月10-18日、石室3次元デジタル計測（高知大学人文学部考古学研究室、[[2006年]]に報告）{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|石室俯瞰図}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室展開図}}]] 埋葬施設としては両袖式[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南西方向に開口する。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006|pp=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}}。 * 石室全長：10.75メートル * 玄室：長さ7.50メートル、幅1.95メートル（奥壁付近）・2.18メートル（玄門付近）、高さ2.85メートル（奥壁付近）・2.84メートル（玄室中央付近）・2.56メートル（玄門付近） * 羨道：長さ3.25メートル、幅1.43メートル、高さ1.85メートル 玄室の床面には割石を敷く。 Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|玄室（奥壁方向） Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|玄室（羨道方向） Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|玄門（羨道方向） Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|羨道（玄室方向） 小蓮古墳 出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|[[高知県立歴史民俗資料館]]展示。}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|出土須恵器
{{small|高知県立歴史民俗資料館展示。}} == 文化財 == === 高知県指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 小蓮古墳 - 1953年（昭和28年）1月29日指定[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm 小蓮古墳]（高知県ホームページ）。。 == 関連施設 == * [[高知県立歴史民俗資料館]]（南国市岡豊町八幡） - 小蓮古墳の出土品を保管・展示。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * 史跡説明板（南国市教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=高知県の地名|series=[[日本歴史地名大系]]40|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490409|ref={{Harvid|小蓮古墳（平凡社）|1983}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=三宅良明|year=1989|chapter=小蓮古墳|title=[[日本古墳大辞典]]|publisher=[[東京堂出版]]|isbn=4490102607|ref={{Harvid|小蓮古墳（古墳）|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|series=高知大学考古学調査研究報告第3冊|publisher=高知大学人文学部考古学研究室|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|南国市における大型後期古墳の調査|2006}}}} - リンクは奈良文化財研究所「全国遺跡報告総覧」。 == 関連項目 == * [[舟岩古墳群]] == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Kohasu Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm 小蓮古墳] - 高知県ホームページ {{DEFAULTSORT:こはすこふん}} [[Category:高知県の古墳]] [[Category:南国市の歴史]] [[Category:高知県指定史跡]]
