Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|12=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|12=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|12=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|ja|高知県立歴史民俗資料館|qq=draft}}.}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery

Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * {{ill|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankoku, Kōchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|ja|南国市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nankoku, Kōchi|qq=}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|ja|高知県|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōchi Prefecture|qq=}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|ja|高知市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōchi, Kōchi|qq=}} and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Sue pottery|qq=}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|ja|古墳時代|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun period|qq=}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

Entrance to the stone chamber | location = [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. {{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Fixed Kofun templates
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History]].}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History]].}} == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kōchi Prefectural Entrance to the stone chamber | location = [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. revision.comment: Text replacement - "市の歴史" to "の Municipal History"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=See as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History]].}} 小蓮古墳出土 須恵器.JPG|Excavated Sue pottery
{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History]].}} == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kōchi Prefectural (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小蓮古墳|version=103486035|comment=Imported full ja So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:01, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = 33.5993 | coordinates = 133.618 | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Nankoku, Kōchi|Nankoku, Kōchi]], [[:en:Kōchi Prefecture|Kōchi Prefecture]], Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] and {{ill|Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meikibikoyama Kofun Cluster|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

{{small|Exhibited at the [[:en:Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History]].}} == Historic Site == === Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' '''Kohasu Kofun''' – Designated on January 29, 1953.[http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] (Kōchi Prefectural website). == Related Facilities == * [[:en:Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History|Kōchi Prefectural Museum of History]] (Nankoku City, Okocho Yawata) – Displays excavated burial goods from Kohasu Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historic (Kofun)|1989}}}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2006|chapter=|url=http://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/9544 |title=Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|series=Kōchi University Kofun}} * [http://www.kochinet.ed.jp/bunkazai/details/410-2/410-2-13.htm Kohasu Kofun] – Kōchi Prefectural Website {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|小蓮古墳|version=103486035|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Koren Kofun]] [[ja:小蓮古墳]] [[Category:Municipal History of Nangoku]] [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Prefectural Historic Sites in Kochi Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Kohasu Kofun}} [[zh:小蓮古墳]]

It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Kōchi, Kōchi|Kōchi, Kōchi]] and {{ill|Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group|ja|明見彦山古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]]. The kofun is dated to the late [[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]], around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones.
