Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|en|Nankoku, Kōchi|WD=Q861621|lt=Nankoku, Kōchi|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.599328|N|133.619147|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|en|Nankoku, Kōchi|WD=Q861621|lt=Nankoku, Kōchi|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|WD=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} and {{ill|Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Burial chamber (rear wall view) Kohasu Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Burial chamber (view towards entrance) Kohasu Kofun, genmon.jpg|Entrance to the burial chamber Kohasu Kofun, sendou-1.jpg|Entrance passage 小蓮古墳 Excavated Artifacts.JPG|excavations

It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|WD=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} and {{ill|Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones. {{Infobox ancient site | name = Kohasu Kofun | image = [[File:Kohasu Kofun, entrance.jpg|280px]]
Entrance to the stone chamber | location = {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|en|Nankoku, Kōchi|qid=Q861621|lt=Nankoku, Kōchi|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|qid=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Japan{{Sfn|Investigation of Large Late Kofun built Tumuli in Nankoku|2006|11=1-4, 13-22, 23-29}} | coordinates = {{Coord|33.599328|N|133.619147|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | type = Unknown | Burial Facility = Double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | built = Late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (6th century latter half) | burial goods = Numerous burial goods, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} | historical_site_Historic Site = Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site "Kohasu Kofun" | map = Japan Kochi | map_dot_label = top }} '''Kohasu Kofun''' (小蓮古墳) is an ancient {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Nankoku, Kōchi|en|Nankoku, Kōchi|qid=Q861621|lt=Nankoku, Kōchi|1=Nankoku, Kōchi}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|qid=Q134093|lt=Kōchi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Japan. It has been Historic Site1 as a historic site by Kōchi Prefecture. Together with {{ill|Asakura Kofun|ja|朝倉古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asakura Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Kōchi, Kōchi|en|Kōchi, Kōchi|qid=Q200251|lt=Kōchi, Kōchi|1=Kōchi, Kōchi}} and {{ill|Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group|ja|明見彦山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myoken Hikoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} No. 1 Kofun in Nankoku, it is collectively referred to as one of the "Three Great Kofun of Tosa." == Overview == Kohasu Kofun is situated at the base of a hillside in western Nankoku, at an elevation of approximately 20 meters. Investigations, including mound surveys and excavations, were conducted in 1972, and a three-dimensional digital survey of the stone chamber was carried out in 2004. The mound is currently elliptical (its original type is uncertain), measuring 28 meters in its north-south major axis, 22 meters in its east-west minor axis, and standing 7.13 meters high. The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening southwest. The stone chamber has been accessible since the Meiji period. The chamber is the largest in Kōchi Prefecture, with an overall length of 10.8 meters. burial goods excavated from the site include hollow gold-co11er beads, gold rings, iron daggers, iron arrowheads, chisels, shells, and {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. The kofun is dated to the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, around the latter half of the 6th century. Its large-map_size stone chamber suggests it served as a central burial site for the regional elite of the Kōchi Plain. The site was Historic Site1 a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site in 1953. == History == * 1953 (Showa 28): Designated as a Kōchi Prefectural Historic Site. * 1972 (Showa 47): Mound survey, stone chamber measurement, and excavation conducted. * 2005 (Heisei 17): 3D digital measurement of the stone chamber conducted by Kōchi University. == burial == [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Kohasu Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility is a double-sleeved {{ill|Yokoana-style stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokoana-style stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area are as follows: * Total length: 10.75 meters * Burial chamber (玄室): ** Length: 7.50 meters ** Width: 1.95 meters (rear wall) to 2.18 meters (entrance) ** Height: 2.85 meters (rear wall) to 2.56 meters (entrance) * Entrance passage (羨道): ** Length: 3.25 meters ** Width: 1.43 meters ** Height: 1.85 meters The floor of the burial chamber is paved with cut stones.
